---
title: "Safety"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Safety}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment   = "#>",
  warning   = FALSE,
  message   = FALSE,
  eval      = interactive()
)
```

This vignette answers the question *how do I share this with untrusted users?* Companion vignettes cover the use-case ladder (`vignette("ggpaintr-use-cases")`), tailoring defaults (`vignette("ggpaintr-customization")`), real ggplot recipes (`vignette("ggpaintr-gallery")`), and LLM integration (`vignette("ggpaintr-llm")`).

## The `expr_check` flag

<!-- TODO: what it does, when (never) to turn it off. -->

## The denylist boundary

<!-- TODO: ~151 entries, treated as complete. AST walker is the actual safety mechanism, not the denylist alone. -->

## Upload trust model

<!-- TODO: what's accepted, where normalization happens, what the host can / can't restrict. -->

## Public-deployment recipe

<!-- TODO: shinyapps.io / Posit Connect checklist; what to lock down at each level. -->

## Trade-off table: L1 vs L2 vs L3

<!-- TODO: deployment surface per level, default settings, what additional surface a host introduces. Strictly ggpaintr-provided surface (host-introduced surfaces only as cautionary side-notes). -->
